hcd_data.architectural_style: House-form, Semi-detached, Bay-and-Gable (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a late-nineteenth- to early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a pair, they retain most of their original building features including cross-gabled rooflines, mirrored facades, double-height bay windows, brick cladding with decorative brickwork and stonework, decorative wooden gable pediments and brackets, and fenestration pattern with flat arch openings, stone sills, and recessed front entrances behind rounded archway. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains supporting the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way EB | 40 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks | Mixed commercial-residential

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include original windows, bay window.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a pair, they retain most of their original building features including cross-gabled rooflines, mirrored facades, double-height bay windows, brick cladding with decorative brickwork and stonework, decorative wooden gable pediments and brackets, and fenestration pattern with flat arch openings, stone sills, and recessed front entrances behind rounded archway. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Three-storey stucco semi-detached (1890-1903) with cross-gable roof. Party walls on both sides. Simple bargeboard. Good condition.
